model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the button in the top-left corner
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (638,124) said dx=-749 dy=-163 conf=0.90 -> (-111,-39)
  iter 1: at (0,0) said dx=54 dy=37 conf=0.90 -> (54,37)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click a button in the top-left corner.
The red crosshair is at approximately (27, 18) based on the text in the bottom right corner, but I should verify this with the image.
The button is a green rectangle in the top-left.
The blue circle is centered on the red crosshair.
The red crosshair is inside the green button.
The green button center seems to be around (45, 37).
The red crosshair is at (27, 18).
Offset from crosshair to button center:
dx = 45 - 27 = 18
dy = 37 - 18 = 19
Let's re-examine the button.
The button is from roughly (0,0) to (92, 70).
Wait, the button is the green rectangle.
The crosshair is at (27, 18) according to the text.
The green rectangle's bounding box: [0, 0, 92, 70].
Center of the green rectangle: (46, 35).
Offset from crosshair (27, 18) to center (46, 35):
dx = 46 - 27 = 19
dy = 35 - 18 = 17
Let's look at the crosshair again. It's at roughly (27, 18).
The button center is roughly (46, 35).
Offset dx ≈ 19, dy ≈ 17.
Confidence should be high since it's clearly on the button but not at the center.
